title: PCs, NCs, and NetPCs — Where Do We Go From Here? (CA Acapulco CIO Conference 1997)
author: Peter S. Kastner
prescience_rationale: Kastner framed the NC/NetPC debate correctly: NC and NetPC did not displace the fat-client PC in general enterprise desktop use; the arguments he highlighted (TCO, centralization, thin-client economics) re-emerged successfully a decade later as VDI, Chromebooks, and web-SaaS.

title: Electronic Commerce: 1998 Practice Summary
author: Aberdeen Group
prescience_rationale: Key predictions proved accurate: internet commerce displaced EDI as dominant B2B protocol; market consolidation occurred as predicted; Microsoft and IBM emerged as platform powers; CSP/outsourcing model became dominant (modern SaaS/cloud); internet advertising exceeded $4B well before 2000.

title: The ESB in the Land of SOA
author: William Mougayar
prescience_rationale: ESB evolution framework was accurate; the convergence toward service/process bus proved correct; governance/registry integration became standard. author: Rich Seeley, SearchWebServices / TechTarget
prescience_rationale: Kastner's 50% Global 5000 BPM engagement statistic was validated by subsequent Gartner/Forrester surveys and directly foreshadowed the 2010s iBPMS / workflow-automation boom (Pegasystems, Appian). title: GoRemote Is Unifying Secure Remote Access Services
author: Peter S. Kastner
prescience_rationale: Aberdeen's prediction that unified managed remote access would displace fragmented a la carte services was directionally correct; the model was later validated by cloud-based SASE/ZTN frameworks.

title: Dell Services — Discussion Materials
author: Peter S. Kastner (Executive VP, Research, Aberdeen Group)
prescience_rationale: Correctly framed services following the manufacturing cost-restructuring arc; hosted/BPO/SaaS all became dominant delivery models post-2010.

title: Why Aberdeen Group is Focusing on Enterprise IT Integration
author: Peter S. Kastner
prescience_rationale: Correctly anticipates SOA becoming the majority development paradigm by ~2010, mainstreaming of mobility, rise of real-time BI/analytics, and server virtualization. The 'network is the computer' / edge processing theme predates cloud/edge computing discourse.

title: Is Sun's SPARC Architecture on Life Support?
author: Peter S. Kastner
prescience_rationale: Rock was cancelled post-merger (2009); Solaris on x86 won commodity share; SPARC business cut by Oracle 2017; Oracle did pursue full-stack strategy with Exadata, Exalogic, SaaS.

title: A Kinder, Gentler Larry Ellison? Oracle Turns New Face to Its User Community
author: Katherine Jones (Aberdeen Group)
prescience_rationale: Accurately anticipated Oracle's long-run move to hosted/managed software (Oracle On Demand → Oracle Cloud); 'daily close' became standard ERP feature; outsourcing/BPO became mainstream.

title: Next Steps in SOA: Applications on the Network
author: Peter S. Kastner
prescience_rationale: The vision of SOA middleware in XML-aware appliances, applications distributed to network edge, and network infrastructure taking on new application processing roles accurately anticipates CDNs, API gateways, edge computing, and cloud-native networking patterns.

title: Real-Time Billing for Internet Protocol (IP) Services
author: International Engineering Consortium (IEC) tutorial
prescience_rationale: Kastner's 'turn on a dime' thesis anticipated SaaS metered billing, cloud pay-as-you-go (AWS 2006+), and modern telco convergent charging. Real-time CM&B became table stakes.

title: Enterprise Integration Technology: Aberdeen Group's Market Trends & Research for 2006
author: Peter S. Kastner
prescience_rationale: Correctly predicted majority SOA adoption within 5 years (by 2011 most enterprises had substantial SOA/web services); accurately forecast that SOA would shift IT budgets toward innovation; the evolution from SOA to microservices/cloud-native validated the core architectural thesis.
